top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on steep paved highway climb, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Mount Baker Hill Climb's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
Mount Baker Hill Climb's steep paved highway climb forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 22 miles.
22 miles on steep paved highway climb burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Base fitness for Mount Baker Hill Climb. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on steep paved highway climb. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,462ft of climbing on steep paved highway climb does to your power file on race day.
203ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Mount Baker Hill Climb, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Mount Baker Hill Climb. Train it until it feels boring.
Below LT1, you burn fat. Above it, you burn glycogen. For 22 miles, a higher LT1 means slower bonk.
At Mount Baker Hill Climb, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.
race_overlay.altitude: Mount Baker Hill Climb with 4,462ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. High-altitude chill post-climb.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 22 miles of steep paved highway climb in Glacier, Washington, USA.
